texto: I don't mind the dark, and because it's Christmas, we've been busy putting lights up everywhere. High, so everyone knows we're okay.
explicacao: Citação de Mary Ann Rivers sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “I don't mind the dark, and because it's Christmas, we've been busy putting lights up everywhere. …” articula amor, christmas, romance em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como natal e reflexao-noturna.

texto: For me, there isn't some miracle cure, this is my life, or my disease will progress and my life will change focus again, and I'll have another new life.I need C to stay right where he is now because for now, I don't know enough to move from where I am.My hypothesis is that the light will come back, both outside and inside me.I'm afraid and angry, but the light is a theory I want to prove.Until then, I just have to keep the experiment going with as many controls as possible.One bus, back and forth.One store.One man, his words under glass.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Mary Ann Rivers sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “For me, there isn't some miracle cure, this is my life, or my disease will progress and my life w…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amor, christmas, romance, útil em momentos de mudanca e natal.

texto: I can't ignore his one-sided almost smile or his methylene blue eyes. I can't ignore his pretty shoulders or his arms. I can't ignore his big hands, his shoulder-blade-spanning hands, the way the tendons in them lock to every knuckle and speculate on things like capability and dexterity and, of course, the scar over those knuckles on his left hand that I've noticed before, and its reminder that he has a life and has been hurt in it.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Mary Ann Rivers: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “I can't ignore his one-sided almost smile or his methylene blue eyes. I can't ignore his pretty s…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, christmas, romance pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de natal e termino.

texto: I wonder if he practices making awkward and nerdy look sort of cool. Like he fills his house with furniture that is the wrong scale for his tall body and buys plaid shirts in bulk and tells his barber to leave crazy, too-long pieces of hair mixed in with the regularly cut hair so everything always looks messy.Then he runs his hands through his hair and puts on his plaid shirts and uses mirrors to watch himself sit in uncomfortable furniture until comfortable furniture looks like it's the one with the problem.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Mary Ann Rivers: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a amor. O trecho “I wonder if he practices making awkward and nerdy look sort of cool. Like he fills his house with…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, christmas, romance pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de natal e romance.

Like he fills his house with furniture that is the wrong scale for his tall body and buys plaid shirts in bulk and tells his barber to leave crazy, too-long pieces of hair mixed in with the regularly cut hair so everything always looks messy.Then he runs his hands through his hair and puts on his plaid shirts and uses mirrors to watch himself sit in uncomfortable furniture until comfortable furniture looks like it's the one with the problem.

texto: I look at my snow boots, counting the grommets while I try to name what I'm feeling. This has been a problem lately. It's never been a problem before—I've been happy, and sad, and frustrated.I've felt angry and sentimental.I've loved. I've been loved back.Maintaining long moments of wordless eye contact with the man who is supposed to make me feel okay about going blind, noticing all the exact shades of blue and how I can always tell he's going to smile before he does, pretending I'm not responding to some tension between us?I'm a little exhausted.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Mary Ann Rivers que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “I look at my snow boots, counting the grommets while I try to name what I'm feeling. This has bee…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, christmas, romance, com foco em natal e termino.

texto: The problem is that stepping away from Brian, leaving him standing under that pergola on Wednesday, is no longer enough to leave behind how he made me feel in that hour. I could leave him there, we could part as strangers, but God, I know that I would look for him. He would live in my peripheral vision, a ghost nudging me to turn and look behind me, only to find a spot that is emptier than empty
explicacao: Citação de Mary Ann Rivers sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “The problem is that stepping away from Brian, leaving him standing under that pergola on Wednesda…” articula amor, letting-go, romance em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como romance.
